title: Kai Justiss
content:

{{Quote|The Wookiee loyalty is legendary. When it most matters, I am sure the Republic can count on their allegiance.|Kai Justiss|<ref name="14:10:03" />}}
'''Kai Justiss''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] during the time surrounding the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and who managed to evade [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] and survive the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. In his early career as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], he encountered a wide variety of creatures in his travels, such as [[marsh haunt]]s and [[muttamok]]s. He also worked with the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] of [[Rwookrrorro/Legends|Rwookrrorro]], [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to thwart a plot by [[Droid/Legends|droid]] specialist [[Turren Lonarr]] to destabilize Wookiee society. When the Clone Wars broke out in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Justiss participated in the first [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battle]], which took place on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]].

He would later serve aboard the [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] ''[[VCD987]]'', alongside fellow Jedi [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]] and [[Sian Jeisel]]. In 22 BBY, the ''VCD987'' was shipping [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]] when it fell under attack from the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. It was subsequently boarded, with Justiss and his companions at the mercy of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], the Confederate leader. Dooku spared them, however, allowing Justiss to continue to serve the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He was permanently assigned to Jedi diplomatic duties on Kashyyyk and was later the target of a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] posted by enemy commander [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]]. When Order 66 was executed, Justiss was forced to flee from agents of the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] that had risen in the Republic's stead, and he was able to elude capture until [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], when he was captured by the Empire and executed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] on board the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]].

==Biography==
===Early career===
[[File:Ursemadu.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Justiss and Du confront a marsh haunt.]]
Kai Justiss, a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], wielded a blue-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. In his work as a Jedi, he encountered many different creatures on far-flung [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. In several of these encounters, such as his meeting a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Jakobeast/Legends|jakobeast]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], he would have to draw his weapon in self-defense. Together with fellow Jedi [[Ur-Sema Du]], he came across yet another Force-sensitive [[Species/Legends|species]] called a [[marsh haunt]]. In one mission, when he used his [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|''Delta''-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor]], his [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] was stolen by [[muttamok]]s, who were known to take any shining object that caught their attention.<ref name="potjs">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> When at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], Justiss visited the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] at least once during [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu's]] tenure there.<ref name="jvs">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

====Mission to Kashyyyk====
At some stage in his early career, Justiss accompanied a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. Landing in [[Rwookrrorro/Legends|Rwookrrorro]], Justiss and his companion were greeted by the translator [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[K-27]]. As the trio began to venture further into the Wookiee [[City/Legends|city]], they were met with the sight and sound of an explosion from a nearby building. As fires raged within the structure, K-27 urged Justiss and the Wookiee to attempt to save anyone inside. Just before Wookiee [[Coruscant Rescue Ops/Legends|firefighters]] arrived, the Jedi and his companion were able to save some of the building's inhabitants.<ref name="kif">Though Kai Justiss only appears in the introductory picture of {{GamerCite|4|Kashyyyk in Flames}}, [[Joe Corroney]] has established in his blog ({{Blog|joecorroney|18|The Story of Kai Justiss}},) that Justiss is a representation of one of the player characters. As such, the biography of the player characters is applied to Justiss' biography here.</ref>

One of the rescued Wookiees, [[Urrurrowo]], explained that there had been [[Flame beetle/Legends|flame beetles]] within the building for unknown reasons. K-27 mentioned that a similar blaze had torn through another part of Rwookrrorro a week prior and that the senior firefighter, [[Rruurrfhurra]], had been hard-pressed to preserve one of the [[Wroshyr tree/Legends|wroshyr]] limbs that the city rested on. Rruurrfhurra arrived shortly afterward; Justiss and his companion mentioned that they had seen some [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droids]] near the building just before the explosion. They then volunteered to help Rruurrfhurra hunt down the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and discover just why the disaster had taken place. K-27, however, warned them that if they interfered with the droids, they would likely be fined and subsequently deported. Rruurrfhurra agreed, but accepted the help of the pair regardless.<ref name="kif" />

In their investigations, Justiss, his companion, and Rruurrfhurra deduced that the flame beetles had been drawn to the walls of the building by what they had perceived to be a large concentration of their hatchlings. This information presented no answers as to how or if the maintenance droids had indeed started the blaze, leaving Justiss and his Wookiee companion with little to do but conclude their business on Kashyyyk. As the Wookiees dealt with the duo's cargo, they discovered the corpse of Rruurrfhurra in one of the containers. Justiss and his ally were subsequently taken into custody, where they explained over the course of several hours their whereabouts since the late firefighter had last been seen. The authorities eventually released the pair, but warned them not to depart Kashyyyk.<ref name="kif" />

[[File:KashyyykInFlamesFinal.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Justiss and his Wookiee ally battle Trandoshans while K-27 watches on.]]
Upon returning to their [[Starship/Legends|ship]], Justiss and his companion found the craft surrounded by [[JR-8 series maintenance droid|JR-8 janitorial droids]] and flame beetles. After removing the threat of the beetles and droids, the pair inspected one of the JR-8s and found that it had come from [[Temporary Droids]]—the rental agency that had supplied the Wookiees with K-27. The protocol droid bristled at the suggestion that his master, [[Turren Lonarr]], could have been involved with any of the recent blazes, but nevertheless informed Justiss that Lonarr had come to Kashyyyk from [[Trandosha/Legends|Dosha]], and that he employed [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]] to guard his Temporary Droids warehouse.<ref name="kif" /> Trandoshans, who hailed from a world that shared a [[Kashyyyk system/Legends|system]] with Kashyyyk, had been engaged in a blood feud with the Wookiees for centuries.<ref name="gb">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

Journeying to the Temporary Droids warehouse, Justiss, his companion, and K-27 were set upon by the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] guards, who were buying Lonarr time to escape. Fighting their way through, Justiss and the Wookiee defeated the Trandoshans, only to see Lonarr escaping in a [[Liftcar/Legends|liftcar]] powered by [[kshyy vine]]s. The pair attempted to override the liftcar controls, forcing Lonarr to jump to relative safety on a wroshyr limb two hundred meters above his assailants. As the Jedi and the Wookiee approached Lonarr's location, they were informed by their quarry that the [[Wookiee nursery ring]], which was just above them, was rigged to attract flame beetles and subsequently be set ablaze if Lonarr died. Justiss and his companion were able to defuse the situation, however, preventing the nursery ring from destruction and defeating Lonarr in the process.<ref name="kif" />

===Clone Wars and aftermath===
[[File:Kaiandchoi.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Justiss, Jeisel, and Choi battle boarders on the ''VCD987''.]]
In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] sent out a distress signal from [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], where he had discovered a droid army being constructed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Kenobi, along with his apprentice [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], had been captured by the Confederacy, and so [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] led a [[Jedi assault team/Legends|team]] of Jedi to Geonosis to save them.<ref name="aotc">[[Star Wars: Episode II — Attack of the Clones (comic series)|''Star Wars: Episode II — Attack of the Clones'' comic adaptation]]</ref> Justiss fought in the ensuing [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] when the brief negotiations failed, alongside many of his fellow Jedi in the [[Petranaki arena]], and was one of the few to survive the battle,<ref name="ttblog">[[Star Wars: Episode II — Attack of the Clones (comic series)|''Star Wars: Episode II — Attack of the Clones'' comic adaptation]] (see: {{Blog|holocron|10|Jedi Casualties: Battle of Geonosis}})</ref> which the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] eventually won.<ref name="aotc" /> As war broke out, Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] agreed to become one of the Republic's military leaders, and Justiss succeeded him as [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]] of Kashyyyk.<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

Seven months into the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Justiss served aboard the [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] ''[[VCD987]]'', which was [[Mission to Drongar|carrying]] reinforcements to [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]].<ref name="j:cd" /> The world was home to the [[Bota/Legends|bota]] fungi, a healing agent that was known to deteriorate quickly when picked. Both the Republic and the Confederacy had harvesting operations there, and so the planet was a contested one.<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Accompanying Justiss were the Jedi [[Sian Jeisel]] and [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]]. In transit, the ''VCD987'' fell under attack from a superior Confederate force, with its engines falling prey to the enemy guns. Justiss, Jeisel and Choi fought alongside [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to repel [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] boarders, but the attackers lowered their guns as [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] boarded the craft, flanked by his [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]].<ref name="j:cd" />

Justiss stood by and watched while Dooku verbally sparred with Jeisel, who had been reluctant to fight for the Republic earlier in the war. The Count insisted that he was not dedicated to the destruction of the Jedi, and to prove it, he had Justiss, Jeisel, and Choi shuttled to neutral territory. The [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] of the ''VCD987'', however, were killed.<ref name="j:cd" /> Later that year, Justiss was permanently assigned to Jedi diplomatic duties for Kashyyyk. In a statement to [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], the Jedi commented on the steadfast reliability of the Wookiees as a people.<ref name="14:10:03" />

[[File:DeathOfKaiJustiss-SWG6.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Justiss executed by Darth Vader]]
In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on Justiss's head by Confederate agent [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]]. The Jedi was not alone, however, as Ventress had placed bounties on the heads of many of his colleagues, such as [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]], [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]], and Anakin Skywalker.<ref name="15:01:15">{{InsiderCite|73|CIS Shadowfeed Dispatch 15:01:15 Edition}}</ref> At the end of the war, Justiss survived [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] and managed to outfox [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] for some time afterward.<ref name="16:5:24">{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}}</ref> For nearly twenty years he evaded [[Death/Legends|death]], and he sought refuge on [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]] circa [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. He was betrayed by local [[Farm/Legends|farmers]] to the [[Crimson Nova]] chapter of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]], who sent a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]—including [[Breela]], [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]], and [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]]—after him. Justiss was forced to battle the hunters and managed to remove at least one [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan's]] arm in the ensuing skirmish.<ref name="uw">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref>

Justiss was eventually captured by the Empire and brought to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] where he was executed at the hands of Darth Vader.<ref name="SWG6" />

==Personality and traits==
Justiss, unlike some such as Sian Jeisel<ref name="j:mw:">''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]''</ref> and [[Serifa Altunen]],<ref name="y:dr">''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''</ref> embraced his role as a Jedi during the Clone Wars.<ref name="j:cd" /> He respected the Wookiees of Kashyyyk, even going so far as to publicly applaud their loyalty on HoloNet News. Justiss was also highly adept at concealing himself, as he was able to avoid the Great Jedi Purge for twenty years.<ref name="uw" /><ref name="16:5:24" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|A long time ago in a magazine far, far away called Star Wars Gamer magazine published by Wizards of the Coast, I created a Jedi character for an illustration who over the past few years has had a small role in the ever-expanding universe of Star Wars.|Joe Corroney|<ref name="blog">{{Blog|joecorroney|18|The Story of Kai Justiss}}</ref>}}
[[File:KaiArchives.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Justiss, as illustrated by Chris Trevas.]]
The character of Kai Justiss was created by artist [[Joe Corroney]] in [[2001]]. Corroney had been tasked with illustrating the role-playing adventure ''[[Kashyyyk in Flames]]'', written by [[J.D. Wiker|J. D. Wiker]], for [[Star Wars Gamer 4|''Star Wars Gamer'' 4]]. His art director, Kyle Hunter, requested that he create depictions for several "player" characters, so as to entice readers. As reference, Corroney based the character on his friend, webmaster, and fellow artist [[Justin Chung]], who had been creating custom lightsabers and [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]].<ref name="blog" />

The character went unnamed in ''Kashyyyk in Flames'', but was later identified in ''[[Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:10:03]]'', written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. The name was coined by Corroney and Chung, and the character was subsequently used in several ensuing installments of [[HoloNet News (Insider)|''HoloNet News'']]. The character also featured in the ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'', and [[John Ostrander]]'s ''[[Jedi: Count Dooku]]'', where Justiss was drawn by [[Jan Duursema]]. Apart from Corroney and Duursema, the only other artist to depict Justiss is [[Chris Trevas]], who rendered the character for [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]. Other authors who contributed to Justiss' story include [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]].<ref name="uw" />

Corroney and Chung would later expand upon the known [[canon]]ical details of Justiss' life in Corroney's blog, "Drawing in the Empire." The expansions were inspired by Justiss' existing depictions and included identifying Ur-Sema Du as Justiss' master, Justiss working directly for Jocasta Nu, Justiss' promotion to the rank of Jedi Master, and Justiss' involvement in the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]]. As these details have not been published in a canonical source, however, they are not canon.<ref name="blog" />
